Raaft.history: Raaft is a SaaS churn-reduction tool that helps subscription companies retain customers who attempt to cancel by presenting targeted cancel flows and gathering cancellation feedback.
Raaft.summary_abuse: Raaft has no notable abuse signal. It is a SaaS cancel-flow and retention tool used by subscription businesses, with no consumer-facing free content hosting surface.

RabbitLoader.history: RabbitLoader was founded in 2019 as an AI-powered WordPress and WooCommerce performance optimisation plugin offering caching, CDN, and image optimisation to improve Core Web Vitals scores.
RabbitLoader.summary_abuse: RabbitLoader has no notable abuse signal. It is a website performance plugin for WordPress with no consumer-facing free hosting surface that fraudsters would exploit.

Rabfy.history: Rabfy is a print-on-demand ecommerce SaaS, allowing merchants to sell custom products without holding inventory. It is a small, recent platform.
Rabfy.summary_abuse: Rabfy is a print-on-demand platform with moderate abuse potential. Print-on-demand services can be exploited to create fake storefronts selling counterfeit or fraudulent products, but there is no specific documented abuse history for Rabfy.

Race Roster.history: Race Roster was founded in 2012 in London, Ontario, Canada by a group of high-school friends after organising a 10K race. In 2019 ASICS Corporation acquired the company for approximately US$28 million to support the global running community.
Race Roster.summary_abuse: Race Roster has no notable abuse signal. It is an event registration and management platform for endurance sports events, with no consumer-facing free hosting surface that fraudsters target.

RackCache.history: Rack::Cache is an open-source Ruby library by Ryan Tomayko, released around 2009, that provides HTTP caching middleware for Rack-based Ruby web applications. It implements HTTP cache semantics and has been widely used in Rails applications.
RackCache.summary_abuse: Rack::Cache is a Ruby caching middleware library with no abuse signal. It is a backend developer tool with no consumer-facing surface.

Ractive.js.history: Ractive.js is a template-driven UI library created around 2013, originally developed at The Guardian newspaper. It pioneered two-way data binding before React popularized the virtual DOM approach. It has since fallen largely out of use as React, Vue, and Angular became dominant.
Ractive.js.summary_abuse: Ractive.js is an open-source JavaScript UI library with no abuse signal. It is a developer tool with no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

Radancy.history: Radancy traces its roots to TMP Worldwide, founded by Andrew McKelvey in 1967 as Telephone Marketing Programs. The company evolved into a digital talent acquisition platform and rebranded as Radancy in January 2021, offering a cloud-based talent acquisition suite including virtual hiring events and career portals.
Radancy.summary_abuse: Radancy has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise talent acquisition and recruitment marketing platform with no consumer-facing free hosting surface fraudsters target.

Radar.history: Radar was founded in 2016 as an all-in-one location platform providing geofencing, maps, and geolocation services primarily for mobile apps and enterprise workflows.
Radar.summary_abuse: Radar has a modest abuse risk profile. It provides location and geofencing APIs that could potentially be misused for tracking individuals without consent, but the platform targets enterprise developers and has standard API access controls.

Radario.history: Radario is a Russia-based ticketing and event management platform designed for event organisers to sell tickets and manage attendees.
Radario.summary_abuse: Radario has a limited abuse risk. As a Russia-based ticketing platform with no broad Western market presence, it has modest exposure to ticket-scam risks typical of ticketing platforms, but no documented large-scale abuse patterns are known.

Radial Ecommerce.history: Radial was formed in 2016 through the merger of Innotrac Corporation and the enterprise services unit of eBay Enterprise, creating an ecommerce fulfillment and order management platform. In 2017, Belgian postal group bpostgroup acquired Radial for $820 million.
Radial Ecommerce.summary_abuse: Radial has no notable abuse signal. It is a large-scale B2B ecommerce fulfillment and order management platform serving major retailers, with no consumer-facing free service that fraudsters exploit.

Radix UI.history: Radix UI was created by WorkOS (formerly Modulz) and first released around 2020. It provides a set of unstyled, accessible React component primitives used as the foundation for popular UI frameworks like shadcn/ui. It quickly gained widespread adoption in the React ecosystem.
Radix UI.summary_abuse: Radix UI is an open-source React component library with no abuse signal. It is a developer tool and has no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

RafflePress.history: RafflePress was founded in 2014 by Syed Balkhi and John Turner as a WordPress giveaway and contest plugin. It is part of the Awesome Motive portfolio of WordPress products which also includes WPForms and OptinMonster.
RafflePress.summary_abuse: RafflePress has a modest abuse risk profile. Giveaway and contest plugins can be used to run fake contests designed to harvest email addresses or social media follows, but RafflePress itself is a legitimate WordPress plugin with published terms of service and no documented large-scale abuse campaigns.

Railway.history: Railway was founded in 2020 by Jake Cooper in San Francisco as a developer-focused cloud deployment platform. In 2024 it raised $100 million to expand its AI-native cloud infrastructure and challenge major cloud providers.
Railway.summary_abuse: Railway is a cloud deployment platform with a free tier that could be used to host malicious applications or phishing infrastructure. Like other PaaS providers it is occasionally misused for hosting malware or C2 infrastructure, but Railway has standard abuse reporting and AUP enforcement typical of developer cloud platforms.

Rain.history: Rain Retail Software was founded in 2008 and is headquartered in Provo, Utah, United States. The company provides a cloud-based point-of-sale, website, and payment platform designed for specialty retailers. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are recorded.
Rain.summary_abuse: Rain is a B2B point-of-sale and retail management platform with no notable abuse signal. Its professional customer base of specialty retailers and subscription model do not create consumer-facing surfaces exploited by fraudsters.

RainLoop.history: RainLoop is an open-source PHP webmail client first released in 2013 under the MIT license. The project was not actively maintained for several years and became effectively abandoned; SnappyMail emerged as the actively maintained community fork.
RainLoop.summary_abuse: RainLoop is a self-hosted webmail client; the software itself has no abuse surface, but abandoned webmail installations running outdated RainLoop versions have historically been targeted by attackers exploiting unpatched vulnerabilities to gain mail server access. No vendor anti-abuse posture applies given the project is unmaintained.

RaiseDonors.history: RaiseDonors was founded in 2014 as an online fundraising platform for nonprofits. In December 2020 it was acquired by Virtuous, a nonprofit CRM provider, and is now integrated into the Virtuous Raise product.
RaiseDonors.summary_abuse: RaiseDonors has no notable abuse signal. It is a nonprofit fundraising platform with no consumer-facing free public hosting surface that fraudsters exploit.

Raisely.history: Raisely was founded in 2016 in Melbourne, Australia as a cloud fundraising platform for nonprofits and charities. It helped over 2,000 organisations raise more than $500M AUD. In May 2023, Raisely was acquired by Aplos and integrated into the Velora nonprofit technology suite.
Raisely.summary_abuse: Raisely has no notable abuse signal. It is a nonprofit fundraising platform with verified charity onboarding, Stripe payment integration, and standard AUP controls. No documented fraud campaigns have been associated with the platform.

Rake.history: Rake is a messaging and live chat platform for customer engagement, combining inbox management and conversation optimisation for businesses.
Rake.summary_abuse: Rake has no notable abuse signal. It is a business messaging and live chat tool with no consumer-facing free content hosting surface fraudsters would exploit.

Raku-uru Cart.history: Raku-uru Cart is a Japanese all-in-one ecommerce platform primarily serving the Japanese domestic market.
Raku-uru Cart.summary_abuse: Raku-uru Cart has no notable abuse signal based on available information. It is a Japanese ecommerce platform with a primarily domestic user base.

Rakuten.history: Rakuten (formerly Ebates) was founded in 1998 in San Francisco as a cash-back rewards and affiliate shopping portal. It was acquired by Rakuten Group (Japan) in 2014 and rebranded from Ebates to Rakuten in 2019.
Rakuten.summary_abuse: Rakuten (the cash-back affiliate portal) has a modest abuse profile. Affiliate link abuse and fraudulent cashback claims have been documented across cash-back platforms generally, but Rakuten has fraud detection systems in place and is a well-established consumer brand with mature AUP enforcement.

Rakuten Advertising.history: Originally founded in 1996 as LinkShare, one of the first affiliate marketing networks. Acquired by Rakuten for $425 million in 2005, rebranded as Rakuten LinkShare, then Rakuten Affiliate Network in 2014, and finally Rakuten Advertising in 2020.
Rakuten Advertising.summary_abuse: Rakuten Advertising operates a large affiliate network and carries standard affiliate ad-network abuse risks including affiliate fraud, cookie stuffing, and forced clicks. As a major network it deploys fraud detection tools and has a compliance team, but affiliate fraud is an inherent industry-wide challenge. Third-party advertiser tracking pixels deployed via the network also carry cross-site data collection risks.

Rakuten Digital Commerce.history: Rakuten Digital Commerce is an enterprise ecommerce platform that is part of the Rakuten Group's broader technology portfolio, offering omnichannel retail solutions.
Rakuten Digital Commerce.summary_abuse: Rakuten Digital Commerce has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise ecommerce platform backed by a large Japanese corporation, sold to retailers with no consumer-facing free hosting tier.

Rally.history: Rally is a CMS platform designed specifically for K-12 schools to build websites and apps with minimal technical overhead.
Rally.summary_abuse: Rally has no notable abuse signal. It is a school website CMS with controlled institutional user base and no consumer-facing free public content hosting.

Rambler.history: Rambler was launched in 1996 by Stack Ltd as a Russian internet portal and web analytics service. It went public in 2005, was acquired by Prof-Media in 2006, and in 2020 Sberbank took full control of Rambler Media Group.
Rambler.summary_abuse: Rambler operates as a Russian internet portal and site rating/analytics service. Being a Russian-operated analytics and ad network, it is not subject to GDPR and Western data protection norms; data collected through its tracking pixels may flow to Russian corporate and state-adjacent entities. No specific documented large-scale fraud abuse patterns, but privacy risk is elevated for non-Russian users.

Ramda.history: Ramda is an open-source functional programming library for JavaScript, first released in 2013. Created by Scott Sauyet and Michael Hurley, it emphasizes immutability, currying, and function composition, and became a widely-adopted utility library in the functional JavaScript community.
Ramda.summary_abuse: Ramda is an open-source JavaScript functional programming library with no abuse signal. It is a developer utility with no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

Ramper.history: Ramper was founded in 2016 in Sao Paulo, Brazil as a B2B sales prospecting and CRM platform targeting SMBs in the Brazilian market.
Ramper.summary_abuse: Ramper has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B sales automation platform with no consumer-facing free hosting surface fraudsters would exploit.

RandemRetail.history: RandemRetail is a multichannel retail management SaaS centralizing operations for online sellers. Limited public history is available.
RandemRetail.summary_abuse: RandemRetail is a multichannel ecommerce management tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant operations tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Raneto.history: Raneto is an open-source Node.js Markdown-powered knowledge base CMS, created around 2014. It stores content as flat Markdown files without a database, making it easy to deploy for documentation sites.
Raneto.summary_abuse: Raneto is an open-source self-hosted knowledge base with no abuse signal. It is a developer tool for documentation and has no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

RankMath SEO.history: Rank Math SEO was developed by MyThemeShop and launched in 2018 as a WordPress SEO plugin. It grew rapidly to become one of the top WordPress SEO plugins, competing directly with Yoast SEO.
RankMath SEO.summary_abuse: Rank Math SEO has no notable abuse signal. It is a WordPress SEO plugin installed on-site by website owners, with no consumer-facing content hosting surface that fraudsters target.

RankScience.history: RankScience was founded in 2017 and participated in Y Combinator's Winter 2017 batch, building an SEO A/B testing SaaS platform for optimizing organic search traffic. In 2024, the company discontinued all software products including the SEO A/B testing platform and ContentEdge AI tool, pivoting to a pure SEO and AI search optimization agency.
RankScience.summary_abuse: RankScience has no notable abuse signal; it was an SEO analytics SaaS tool with no consumer-facing hosting or user-generated content features that could be exploited for phishing or fraud.

Raphael.history: Raphael.js was created by Dmitry Baranovskiy in 2008, originally developed in collaboration with Sencha Labs, as a cross-browser JavaScript library for drawing vector graphics using SVG and VML. It was a pioneering tool for browser-compatible vector graphics before SVG became universally supported. The project has seen minimal activity in recent years as SVG native support and newer libraries like D3.js have supplanted it.
Raphael.summary_abuse: Raphael.js has no notable abuse signal; it is a client-side JavaScript graphics library with no server-side component or hosted service surface that could be targeted for abuse.

Rapid Active CRM.history: Rapid Active CRM is a sales and marketing automation SaaS targeting small businesses. It is a small platform with limited public history.
Rapid Active CRM.summary_abuse: Rapid Active CRM is a sales and marketing automation tool with no notable abuse signal. CRM platforms can be misused for spam, but there is no specific documented abuse history for this product.

Rapid Search.history: Rapid Search is an AI-powered Shopify search and filter app launched around 2020. It is a Shopify-native product with no notable ownership events.
Rapid Search.summary_abuse: Rapid Search is a Shopify search app with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing product enhancement tool with no documented history of exploitation.

RapidSec.history: RapidSec was founded in 2020 in Israel as an automated client-side security monitoring platform focused on Content Security Policy (CSP). In January 2022, Orca Security acquired RapidSec for approximately $5 million.
RapidSec.summary_abuse: RapidSec has no notable abuse signal. It is a security monitoring tool that helps websites detect client-side attacks; it is a defensive technology with no known use in abusive contexts.

RapidSpike.history: RapidSpike was founded in 2015 by Andrew Mason and Robin Hill in Leeds, England as a website uptime and performance monitoring service for digital teams.
RapidSpike.summary_abuse: RapidSpike has no notable abuse signal. It is a website monitoring and performance tool used by developer and marketing teams, with no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

RapidWeaver.history: RapidWeaver was first released in 2004 by Realmac Software, a UK company founded in November 2002 by Dan Counsell. It became one of the most popular website builder applications for macOS, generating websites locally on the user's machine.
RapidWeaver.summary_abuse: RapidWeaver has no notable abuse signal. It is a Mac website builder that generates static websites locally; it has no hosted platform or free content hosting surface that fraudsters would exploit.

Raptive.history: AdThrive was founded in 2013 by Kelly and Andrew Wilder as a premium ad network for lifestyle bloggers. CafeMedia acquired AdThrive in 2016, and the combined entity rebranded as Raptive in April 2023, positioning itself as a creator-focused advertising company serving over 4,000 publishers and reaching 195 million monthly unique visitors.
Raptive.summary_abuse: Raptive is an ad-serving and revenue management network for independent publishers. As an ad-tech platform, it delivers third-party advertising code onto publisher sites, which carries inherent tracker density and data-collection risk. Raptive does not expose consumer-facing hosting or content services that would make it a target for phishing or scam infrastructure. Its primary abuse risk is the standard ad-tech vector of tracking across publisher sites.

Raptor.history: Raptor Services was founded in 2013 in Denmark as a personalisation and customer data platform using machine learning to power recommendation engines and cross-channel personalisation for retailers.
Raptor.summary_abuse: Raptor has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B personalisation and CDP platform for ecommerce, with no consumer-facing free hosting surface that fraudsters would exploit.

Rasayel.history: Rasayel was founded around 2020 and is registered in Dublin, Ireland, with a distributed team across multiple countries. The company is a Meta Business Partner providing a WhatsApp-first B2B sales and messaging platform that helps companies sell and grow through WhatsApp conversations. No major acquisitions are recorded.
Rasayel.summary_abuse: Rasayel is a WhatsApp business messaging platform. As with any messaging infrastructure tool, there is inherent risk of misuse for unsolicited commercial messaging or spam through WhatsApp. However, the platform targets verified B2B sales teams operating as a Meta Business Partner, which provides some guardrails against the most blatant abuse. No specific documented incidents of large-scale misuse have been reported.

Raspbian.history: Raspbian (now rebranded as Raspberry Pi OS) was first released in 2012 as a Debian-based Linux distribution optimized for the Raspberry Pi single-board computer, developed by Mike Thompson and Peter Green. It became the official operating system for Raspberry Pi hardware, maintained by the Raspberry Pi Foundation, and was renamed Raspberry Pi OS in 2020.
Raspbian.summary_abuse: Raspbian has no notable abuse signal; it is a desktop and embedded Linux OS distributed freely for educational and maker hardware, with no hosted service component that could be exploited for fraud.

RateParity.history: RateParity is a conversion rate optimization and rate management SaaS for hotels, helping properties maintain price consistency across booking channels.
RateParity.summary_abuse: RateParity is a hotel CRO tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B hospitality product with no documented history of exploitation.

Ratesight.history: Ratesight is an online review management SaaS helping businesses collect and track customer reviews. It is a niche product with limited public history.
Ratesight.summary_abuse: Ratesight is a review management tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool for reputation management with no documented history of exploitation.

Rawabit.history: Rawabit is a drag-and-drop landing page and website builder SaaS targeting small businesses. It is a small, recent platform with limited public history.
Rawabit.summary_abuse: Rawabit is a freemium website and landing page builder with moderate abuse potential. Free page builders with custom subdomain hosting are frequently exploited for phishing pages and fake storefronts, though no specific documented incidents exist for Rawabit.

RawGit.history: RawGit was created by Ryan Grove as a free service that served raw files from GitHub repositories with correct HTTP Content-Type headers, filling a gap where GitHub's raw file serving set headers preventing direct use in browsers. The service shut down permanently on October 15, 2019, after GitHub and jsDelivr provided better alternatives.
RawGit.summary_abuse: RawGit was occasionally misused to host malicious scripts through GitHub repositories, since it served any public GitHub file directly to browsers. However, it shut down in 2019 and is no longer operational; any sites still referencing rawgit.com CDN URLs will receive no content.

Raychat.history: Raychat was founded in 2017 in Tabriz, Iran as a free live chat and customer messaging platform. In January 2021, Raychat suffered a major data breach in which approximately 150 million user records were exposed due to a misconfigured MongoDB server and subsequently destroyed by an automated bot attack.
Raychat.summary_abuse: Raychat has a documented security failure: in 2021 a misconfigured database exposed roughly 150 million user records including names, emails, hashed passwords and Telegram IDs, which were then wiped by a meow bot. The platform is Iran-based and not subject to GDPR or equivalent Western privacy frameworks, creating elevated privacy risk. No large-scale fraud abuse of the platform itself is documented beyond the data breach.

Raygun.history: Raygun was founded in 2012 in Wellington, New Zealand, as a crash reporting and application performance monitoring SaaS. It has raised modest funding from Punakaiki Fund and remains an independent bootstrapped company focused on software observability.
Raygun.summary_abuse: Raygun is an error-monitoring and APM SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It collects application telemetry from developer-integrated SDKs with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Rayo.history: Rayo is a CMS platform with its official website at rayo.ir, suggesting Iranian origin. It provides tools for creating and managing websites, blogs, and e-commerce stores. Limited public information is available about the founding team or ownership history.
Rayo.summary_abuse: Rayo is a niche regional CMS platform with no documented abuse signal in public security research. Its limited international footprint and B2B focus mean it is not commonly exploited for phishing or consumer-facing fraud.

Razorpay.history: Razorpay was founded in 2014 by Harshil Mathur and Shashank Kumar, IIT Roorkee alumni, in Bangalore, India. Born out of frustration with legacy bank payment gateways, they joined Y Combinator and built India's first developer-friendly payment gateway for startups and SMBs. The company has grown into one of India's leading fintech platforms, expanding from payment processing to banking, payroll, and lending services.
Razorpay.summary_abuse: Razorpay is occasionally implicated in payment fraud schemes targeting Indian merchants, but legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates. The company maintains PCI DSS Level 1 compliance, ISO 27001 certification, and active fraud detection. As a payment gateway handling significant transaction volumes, it faces standard payment fraud risks but has dedicated risk and compliance teams and a published AUP.

RBS Change.history: RBS Change is a French open-source CMS and e-commerce platform developed by the Strasbourg company RBS starting in 2006, open-sourced under AGPL in 2012. Following RBS judicial liquidation, Proximis took over its development and later Click & Mortar ensured maintenance. The platform is mainly used in France but has largely faded relative to competitors like PrestaShop and Magento.
RBS Change.summary_abuse: RBS Change is a legacy French open-source CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is a niche self-hosted platform with very limited active use and no documented fraud pattern.

RCMS.history: RCMS (Relational Content Management System) is a Japanese CMS developed by Diverta Inc., with development originating around 2004 and the company formally founded in 2005 in Tokyo. It originated from a website built for a Waseda University sports club.
RCMS.summary_abuse: RCMS has no notable abuse signal. It is a Japanese enterprise CMS platform with a primarily domestic user base and no consumer-facing features that fraudsters exploit.

Rcode Vision.history: Rcode Vision is a Tunisian web application development company based in Sousse. The company specialises in building e-commerce platforms, showcase websites, travel agency sites, and hotel reservation systems for regional clients. The founding year is not publicly disclosed.
Rcode Vision.summary_abuse: Rcode Vision is a small Tunisian web development company with no documented abuse signal. Its bespoke development services model does not create a consumer-facing platform surface that would be exploited for phishing or fraud at scale.

RD Station.history: Founded in 2011 in Florianopolis, Brazil by Eric Santos and co-founders as Resultados Digitais, RD Station became Brazil's dominant marketing automation platform. In 2021, TOTVS acquired the company for approximately 1.86 billion reais, the largest private software M&A deal in Brazil's history at the time.
RD Station.summary_abuse: RD Station has no notable abuse signal. It is a marketing automation platform primarily serving Latin American SMBs, with standard email and lead management features. As a marketing email sender it may occasionally be used for spam but has standard AUP enforcement.

RDoc.history: RDoc is Ruby built-in documentation generation tool, included with Ruby since early versions around 2001. It parses Ruby source code and produces HTML and command-line documentation. It is maintained as part of the Ruby standard library and ecosystem.
RDoc.summary_abuse: RDoc is a Ruby documentation generation tool with no abuse signal. It is a developer tool with no consumer-facing surface and is not used in abusive contexts.

RE-OS.com.history: RE-OS.com is a real estate CRM and MLS management SaaS for real estate professionals. It is a specialized B2B product with limited public history.
RE-OS.com.summary_abuse: RE-OS.com is a real estate CRM and MLS platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a professional B2B tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Re:amaze.history: Re:amaze was founded in 2012 as a multi-brand customer service and helpdesk platform for ecommerce businesses. In April 2021, GoDaddy acquired Re:amaze to integrate it into its suite of commerce tools for small businesses.
Re:amaze.summary_abuse: Re:amaze is a customer service platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B helpdesk tool without consumer-facing content hosting or custom domain features that would attract phishing or scam infrastructure.

RE:Guest.history: RE:Guest (reguest.io) is a hotel marketing automation SaaS for increasing direct bookings and online visibility. It is a hospitality-sector product with limited public history.
RE:Guest.summary_abuse: RE:Guest is a hotel marketing SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool for hospitality businesses with no documented history of exploitation.

Re:plain.history: Re:plain is a live chat widget that routes conversations to WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, and Telegram, making it popular with Russian and Eastern European small businesses.
Re:plain.summary_abuse: Re:plain has a modest abuse profile. As a live chat tool routing to personal messaging apps, it can be used by fraudulent sites to establish a veneer of legitimacy while routing inquiries to unverified contacts. No documented large-scale fraud campaigns are associated with the platform.

React.history: React was created by Jordan Walke at Facebook in 2011 as an internal tool to handle Facebook's complex news feed UI. It was open-sourced at JSConf US in May 2013. Initially met with skepticism over JSX syntax, it became the dominant frontend UI library within a few years. In October 2025, Meta announced it would donate React, React Native, and JSX to the React Foundation under the Linux Foundation, which officially launched on February 24, 2026.
React.summary_abuse: React is a foundational open-source UI library with no notable abuse signal. It is a client-side rendering library with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters target directly.

React Bricks.history: React Bricks is a visual CMS built on top of React, designed to let developers define editable React components that content editors can manage visually. Founded circa 2020 by an Italian team and aimed at React-based headless CMS workflows.
React Bricks.summary_abuse: React Bricks is a niche developer-focused CMS with no notable abuse signal. Its architecture requires developer integration and does not expose anonymous content hosting.

React Flow.history: React Flow is an open-source MIT-licensed library for building node-based graph UIs in React, originally created by the team at webkid.io in Germany around 2019. It has gained significant traction for building workflow editors, diagramming tools, and visual programming interfaces.
React Flow.summary_abuse: React Flow is a developer library for building node-based graph interfaces and has no consumer-facing surface. It has no notable abuse signal.

React Native for Web.history: React Native for Web was created by Nicolas Gallagher at Twitter (now X) around 2015 to enable sharing React Native component code across web and native platforms. It powers Twitter's web client and has been used by various companies for cross-platform development.
React Native for Web.summary_abuse: React Native for Web is an open-source developer library with no notable abuse signal. It is a UI compatibility layer with no consumer-facing surface.

React Redux.history: React Redux is the official React binding for Redux, the popular JavaScript state management library. It was created alongside Redux by Dan Abramov and maintained by the Redux team. Redux itself was introduced at React Europe 2015.
React Redux.summary_abuse: React Redux is a foundational state management library with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

React Router.history: React Router was created by Ryan Florence and Michael Jackson in 2014 as the standard routing library for React applications. In 2022 it merged with the Remix framework team, and the Remix company was subsequently acquired by Shopify. React Router v6 and v7 adopt Remix conventions directly, making the boundary between router and framework increasingly thin.
React Router.summary_abuse: React Router is a foundational client-side routing library for React with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a build dependency with no consumer-facing surface.

Reactful.history: Reactful is a website visitor engagement and personalisation platform that responds to visitor intent signals in real time.
Reactful.summary_abuse: Reactful has no notable abuse signal. It is a website visitor personalisation tool with no consumer-facing free hosting surface.

Reactive.history: Reactive is a subscription ecommerce and CMS SaaS built on Ruby on Rails, targeting retail, hospitality, and food service businesses.
Reactive.summary_abuse: Reactive is a retail and hospitality ecommerce SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing platform with no documented history of exploitation.

Read the Docs.history: Read the Docs was founded in 2010 as an open-source community project by Eric Holscher, Charles Leifer, and Bobby Grace to host documentation built with Sphinx. It became the de-facto home for Python and open-source project documentation. The community version (readthedocs.org) remains free while a commercial offering (readthedocs.com) was launched for private and business use.
Read the Docs.summary_abuse: Read the Docs is a documentation hosting platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves technical documentation for open-source and commercial projects with no consumer-facing surface commonly exploited for fraud.

ReadAloud.history: ReadAloud (readaloudwidget.com) is a SiteSpeaker text-to-speech widget service that converts web content to audio for accessibility and content consumption.
ReadAloud.summary_abuse: ReadAloud is a text-to-speech widget with no notable abuse signal. It is an accessibility-focused content tool with no documented history of exploitation.

ReadMe.history: ReadMe was founded in 2014 in San Francisco as a developer documentation and API portal platform. It raised Series B funding and expanded to support interactive API documentation with live API explorers, becoming widely used by API-first companies.
ReadMe.summary_abuse: ReadMe is a developer documentation SaaS platform with no notable abuse signal. It hosts technical API documentation with no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

ReadSpeaker.history: ReadSpeaker is a Swedish text-to-speech SaaS founded in 1999, offering web-based audio conversion of text content. It serves publishers, educational institutions, and public-sector organizations seeking accessibility tools.
ReadSpeaker.summary_abuse: ReadSpeaker is a text-to-speech accessibility widget with no notable abuse signal. It converts text to audio for website visitors and has no features commonly exploited for fraud.

Readymag.history: Readymag is a browser-based design tool for creating websites, portfolios, and presentations, founded around 2012. It is aimed at designers and creative professionals who prefer a visual approach over coding.
Readymag.summary_abuse: Readymag is a design-focused website builder with a free tier. It allows users to publish content publicly, which could theoretically host phishing pages, though no significant documented abuse patterns have been reported for this platform.

ReadyPlanet.history: ReadyPlanet is a Thai marketing platform offering website building, CRM, and e-commerce tools, primarily serving small and medium Thai businesses since the early 2000s.
ReadyPlanet.summary_abuse: ReadyPlanet is a Thai marketing platform with no documented abuse signal. It serves local Thai small businesses and has no internationally notable abuse pattern.

Reaktion.history: Reaktion is an ecommerce optimization platform providing solutions to help businesses improve online profits. It is a German-market product with no notable ownership events.
Reaktion.summary_abuse: Reaktion is an ecommerce solutions platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Real Geeks.history: Real Geeks is a US-based SaaS platform for real estate professionals providing CRM, IDX website, and lead generation tools. Founded around 2008, it serves real estate agents and brokers primarily in North America.
Real Geeks.summary_abuse: Real Geeks is a real estate CRM and website platform with no notable abuse signal. It is sold to licensed real estate professionals and does not expose consumer-facing features commonly exploited for fraud.

Realist.history: Realist is a Turkish website tracking and link exchange platform. Link exchange platforms have historically been used for SEO manipulation.
Realist.summary_abuse: Realist is a link exchange and site tracker from Turkey. Link exchange platforms are frequently exploited for SEO manipulation and spam link schemes. The link exchange category has a well-documented history of use in black-hat SEO practices.

Really Simple CAPTCHA.history: Really Simple CAPTCHA is a free WordPress plugin created by Takayuki Miyoshi (Japan) in 2008, designed to be used alongside Contact Form 7 as a simple CAPTCHA implementation. It has been widely used due to its integration with Contact Form 7.
Really Simple CAPTCHA.summary_abuse: Really Simple CAPTCHA is an anti-spam tool for WordPress and has no notable abuse signal. It is itself used to prevent automated abuse on contact forms.

Really Simple SSL & Security.history: Really Simple SSL & Security is a popular WordPress plugin founded by Rogier Lankhorst in the Netherlands around 2014, designed to simplify SSL certificate activation and provide basic security hardening for WordPress sites. It has grown to over 6 million active installations.
Really Simple SSL & Security.summary_abuse: Really Simple SSL & Security is a WordPress security plugin with no notable abuse signal. It adds SSL enforcement and security headers to WordPress sites and is not associated with fraud or spam.

RealNex MarketPlace.history: RealNex was founded in 2014 by the Pavonis Group through the acquisitions of multiple commercial real estate technology companies including REA, Property Line, REI Wise, Ten-Eight, and BuzzTarget. The integrated platform provides CRM, analytics, presentation, transaction management, and marketplace tools for commercial real estate professionals. RealNex celebrated its 10th anniversary in 2024.
RealNex MarketPlace.summary_abuse: RealNex MarketPlace is a commercial real estate software platform with no notable abuse signal. Its professional B2B customer base of commercial real estate brokers and brokerages and subscription pricing model mean it is not exploited for consumer-facing fraud.

RealSatisfied.history: RealSatisfied is an Australian client feedback and review platform for real estate professionals, designed to collect post-transaction client surveys and display testimonials publicly.
RealSatisfied.summary_abuse: RealSatisfied is a real estate review collection platform with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a B2B tool for licensed real estate agents.

Realytics.history: Realytics is a French TV advertising analytics platform that tracks which TV commercials drive online traffic spikes in real time, serving advertisers and media agencies.
Realytics.summary_abuse: Realytics is a TV advertising analytics SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B analytics tool for media agencies with no consumer-facing surface.

RebelMouse.history: RebelMouse is a CMS platform founded in 2012 by Paul Berry (former CTO of Huffington Post), targeting media companies and publishers. It focuses on social-first content distribution.
RebelMouse.summary_abuse: RebelMouse is a publishing CMS targeting media companies with no notable abuse signal. It requires paid enterprise contracts and is not accessible for anonymous or low-effort fraudulent use.

Rebuy.history: Rebuy is a Shopify e-commerce personalization and recommendation engine platform, founded around 2019, that helps merchants increase average order value through AI-driven product recommendations.
Rebuy.summary_abuse: Rebuy is an e-commerce personalization SaaS for Shopify merchants with no notable abuse signal.

REC.history: REC is a niche commercial SaaS product with limited public documentation available.
REC.summary_abuse: REC has no notable abuse signal based on available information.

reCAPTCHA.history: reCAPTCHA was created by Luis von Ahn and colleagues at Carnegie Mellon University and launched in 2007 as a CAPTCHA system that simultaneously helped digitize books and newspapers. Google acquired reCAPTCHA in 2009 and has since evolved it through multiple versions: v1 (text-based), v2 (checkbox/image challenge), and v3 (invisible score-based), integrating it with Google's machine learning infrastructure.
reCAPTCHA.summary_abuse: reCAPTCHA is a security service designed to stop automated abuse, so its own abuse profile is low. It is occasionally bypassed by CAPTCHA-solving services and farm-based human solvers used by spammers, but this is an attack on reCAPTCHA rather than abuse through it. Google maintains active development to stay ahead of bypass techniques. The service collects user interaction data which is used to improve Google's risk analysis systems.

ReCaptcha v2 for Contact Form 7.history: ReCaptcha v2 for Contact Form 7 is a WordPress plugin that restores reCaptcha v2 support to Contact Form 7 after CF7 v5.1 dropped it in December 2018; it was created to fill that specific compatibility gap.
ReCaptcha v2 for Contact Form 7.summary_abuse: This is a WordPress plugin restoring reCaptcha v2 to Contact Form 7 after upstream dropped support. It has no notable abuse signal and is itself an anti-spam and anti-abuse tool.

Recapture.history: Recapture was founded in 2015 by David Webber, John Webber, and Dave Rodenbaugh and is based in the United States. The company provides an abandoned cart email and SMS recovery platform for ecommerce merchants on Shopify, Magento, and other platforms. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are recorded.
Recapture.summary_abuse: Recapture is an ecommerce email and SMS marketing platform focused on abandoned cart recovery. As an email marketing tool, it carries a low to moderate risk of being used to send unsolicited promotional messages; however, it targets ecommerce merchants and requires legitimate store accounts. No significant documented incidents of abuse have been reported.

Recart.history: Recart is an e-commerce messaging platform founded around 2016, initially focused on Facebook Messenger cart abandonment before expanding to SMS and other channels.
Recart.summary_abuse: Recart is an e-commerce abandoned cart messaging platform. It sends cart recovery messages via Facebook Messenger and SMS, which could occasionally be perceived as spam if merchant opt-in practices are poor, but no specific documented abuse incidents are recorded.

Recent Posts Widget With Thumbnails.history: Recent Posts Widget With Thumbnails is a free WordPress plugin extending the default Recent Posts widget with thumbnail image support, maintained by a single developer since around 2012.
Recent Posts Widget With Thumbnails.summary_abuse: Recent Posts Widget With Thumbnails has no notable abuse signal. It is a simple WordPress display widget with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Recharge.history: Recharge was founded in 2014 in Santa Monica, California, as the leading subscription payments platform for Shopify merchants. It raised over $300 million in funding and became the de-facto subscription billing solution for direct-to-consumer brands.
Recharge.summary_abuse: Recharge is a subscription billing platform for e-commerce with no notable abuse signal. It handles recurring payments for legitimate DTC brands with PCI DSS compliance and standard fraud detection.

Recharts.history: Recharts is an open-source MIT-licensed React charting library first released in 2016, built on top of D3.js. It has become one of the most popular chart libraries in the React ecosystem.
Recharts.summary_abuse: Recharts is a React charting library with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Recite Me.history: Recite Me is a UK-based web accessibility overlay solution founded around 2010, providing text-to-speech, translation, and reading aids for website visitors with disabilities.
Recite Me.summary_abuse: Recite Me is a web accessibility overlay tool with no notable abuse signal. It assists users with disabilities in consuming web content and has no features commonly exploited for fraud.

Recolize.history: Recolize is a German e-commerce product recommendation engine SaaS founded around 2012, providing AI-driven personalized product recommendations for online stores.
Recolize.summary_abuse: Recolize is a product recommendation SaaS for e-commerce with no notable abuse signal.

Recombee.history: Recombee was founded in 2015 in Prague, Czech Republic by Pavel Kordik, Tomas Rehorek, Ondrej Fiedler, and Antonin Kral as an AI-powered recommendation-as-a-service platform with a RESTful API and SDKs.
Recombee.summary_abuse: Recombee is a backend recommendation API service with no notable abuse signal. It provides recommendations via API integration and has no consumer-facing content hosting surface that would be targeted for phishing or spam.

Recomify.history: Recomify is a Shopify product recommendation engine, installed with one click. It is a small Shopify app with no notable ownership events.
Recomify.summary_abuse: Recomify is a product recommendation tool for Shopify stores with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing enhancement with no documented history of exploitation.

Recommy.history: Recommy is a customer feedback and survey SaaS for collecting and analyzing user input. It is a small product with limited public history.
Recommy.summary_abuse: Recommy is a customer feedback collection tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B survey and feedback tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Reconline.history: Reconline is a cloud-based hotel reservation and channel management system primarily serving European hotels.
Reconline.summary_abuse: Reconline has no notable abuse signal. It is a hotel reservation SaaS with no consumer-facing free hosting surface fraudsters target.

ReConvert.history: ReConvert (now branded as Upsell.com) is a Shopify app for post-purchase upselling and thank-you page optimisation. It launched around 2018 and grew to become one of the top-rated upsell apps on the Shopify App Store.
ReConvert.summary_abuse: ReConvert has no notable abuse signal as a platform. It is a Shopify post-purchase upsell tool; individual merchants using it could employ dark patterns in their upsell flows, but the app itself is a legitimate Shopify marketplace product with visible pricing and standard review oversight.

Recooty Job Widget.history: Recooty is an India-based job board and recruiting software that provides a widget for embedding live job listings on company websites.
Recooty Job Widget.summary_abuse: Recooty Job Widget has no notable abuse signal. It is a recruiting embeddable widget with no consumer-facing free content hosting.

Recop.history: Recop is a Japanese recruitment management system provided by HR Solutions (hr-s.co.jp) and marketed as Japan's best-selling part-time staff recruitment ATS. The platform covers the full part-time hiring workflow including application management, interview scheduling, and onboarding. HR Solutions also developed an expanded successor platform called Hisol for broader employee management. No significant ownership changes are recorded.
Recop.summary_abuse: Recop is a Japanese B2B recruitment software platform with no documented abuse signal. Its enterprise HR customer base and closed SaaS model make it unsuitable as a vehicle for phishing or consumer-facing fraud.

RecoverMyCart.history: RecoverMyCart is a Shopify abandoned cart recovery app helping merchants re-engage customers who left without purchasing.
RecoverMyCart.summary_abuse: RecoverMyCart has no notable abuse signal. It is a Shopify cart recovery tool used by legitimate ecommerce merchants.

Recras.history: Recras was founded in 2012 and is based in Groningen, the Netherlands. The company provides smart administration and reservation software specifically designed for recreation and leisure companies, covering booking, capacity management, and invoicing. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are recorded.
Recras.summary_abuse: Recras is a niche Dutch booking and administration platform for leisure companies with no documented abuse signal. Its closed B2B deployment model and industry-specific focus mean it is not associated with phishing, spam, or consumer fraud.

Recruitee.history: Recruitee is a Dutch applicant tracking system (ATS) founded in 2015 in Amsterdam. It was acquired by Cooperative Computing and integrated with their HR software suite.
Recruitee.summary_abuse: Recruitee is a B2B applicant tracking system with no notable abuse signal. It serves HR departments at companies and does not expose consumer-facing features commonly exploited for fraud.

Recruition.history: Recruition is a Dutch recruitment management SaaS for HR teams. It is a Netherlands-based B2B HR product with no notable ownership events.
Recruition.summary_abuse: Recruition is a Dutch recruitment platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a professional B2B HR tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Recurate.history: Recurate is a US-based recommerce and branded resale platform founded around 2020, enabling brands to run peer-to-peer resale programs directly on their own sites.
Recurate.summary_abuse: Recurate is a brand-operated resale platform with no notable abuse signal. It enables verified brand resale programs rather than open marketplaces, which limits abuse exposure.

Recurly.history: Recurly was founded in 2009 in San Francisco as a subscription billing and revenue management platform. It has raised over $100 million in funding and serves hundreds of subscription businesses, competing with Stripe Billing and Chargebee.
Recurly.summary_abuse: Recurly is a subscription billing platform with no notable abuse signal beyond standard payment fraud risks. It maintains PCI DSS compliance and SOC 2 Type 2 certification and has dedicated fraud prevention tooling.

Red Hat.history: Red Hat was founded in 1993 by Bob Young and Marc Ewing in Durham, North Carolina, and became the world's first billion-dollar open-source company. It pioneered the enterprise Linux subscription model, distributing Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL). IBM acquired Red Hat in 2019 for $34 billion, one of the largest software acquisitions in history.
Red Hat.summary_abuse: Red Hat Enterprise Linux is a foundational enterprise operating system with no notable abuse signal beyond what any general-purpose OS faces. It is managed by IBM with robust security programs.

Red Hat Gluster.history: GlusterFS was created by Gluster Inc. in 2005 as an open-source distributed network filesystem. Red Hat acquired Gluster Inc. in 2011 for approximately $136 million to integrate distributed storage into its enterprise offerings.
Red Hat Gluster.summary_abuse: Red Hat Gluster (GlusterFS) is a distributed network filesystem with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Red je Pakketje.history: Red je Pakketje is a Dutch same-day delivery logistics company serving online retailers in the Netherlands.
Red je Pakketje.summary_abuse: Red je Pakketje is a Dutch last-mile delivery service with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a B2B carrier integration for e-commerce merchants.

REDAXO.history: REDAXO is a German open-source PHP CMS first released around 2004, popular in the German-speaking web agency market. It is community-maintained and has evolved steadily with an active developer community in DACH region. A major version 5 rewrite was released around 2015.
REDAXO.summary_abuse: REDAXO is a self-hosted PHP CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is used by German-speaking web agencies for client websites and has no centralized infrastructure exploitable for fraud.

RedCart.history: RedCart was founded in 2008 and is headquartered in Szczawno-Zdroj, Poland. The company provides a SaaS-based multi-channel ecommerce platform targeting Polish and Central European merchants, enabling sales across marketplaces such as Amazon, eBay, and Allegro, and integrating with Google Merchant. The company has grown to approximately 14 employees and around $1.5M in annual revenue. No major acquisitions are recorded.
RedCart.summary_abuse: RedCart is a Polish ecommerce SaaS platform with a relatively low abuse profile. Like all ecommerce platforms, fraudulent merchants could potentially use it to set up fake storefronts, but its regional market focus and subscription model limit the breadth of misuse. No significant documented incidents of abuse have been reported.

Reddit.history: Reddit was founded in 2005 by Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian at the University of Virginia. Conde Nast acquired it in 2006. It became independent in 2011 and went public (NYSE: RDDT) in March 2024. It is one of the most-visited websites globally, structured around user-created communities called subreddits.
Reddit.summary_abuse: Reddit has documented history of abuse including spam campaigns, disinformation, scam promotions in subreddits, and coordinated vote manipulation. Its open community model with free anonymous accounts creates opportunities for promotion of scams and fraud. Reddit has banned many subreddits for policy violations and has an active trust and safety team, but enforcement is inconsistent across the platform's vast scale.

Reddit Ads.history: Reddit Ads is the self-serve advertising platform of Reddit Inc., launched formally as a business product around 2009. It allows advertisers to place sponsored posts and banner ads across Reddit subreddits, with targeting based on community and interest.
Reddit Ads.summary_abuse: Reddit Ads is an ad platform with documented cases of fraudulent advertisers running scam promotions, crypto pump schemes, and fake investment opportunities. Reddit reviews ads but enforcement is imperfect given scale. Legitimate use dominates but the platform has lower ad verification barriers than some competitors.

Redicom.history: Redicom is a Portuguese ecommerce platform provider offering premium ecommerce solutions primarily to the Portuguese market.
Redicom.summary_abuse: Redicom has no notable abuse signal. It is a Portuguese ecommerce platform for businesses with no consumer-facing free hosting.

RediRedi.history: RediRedi is an AI-powered sales intelligence and product catalog SaaS for ecommerce. It is a recent product with limited public history.
RediRedi.summary_abuse: RediRedi is a sales intelligence SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B ecommerce tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Redis.history: Redis was created by Salvatore Sanfilippo (antirez) in 2009 while solving scalability problems at an Italian startup. Redis Labs was founded in 2011 to build commercial products around Redis. In 2018, Redis Labs acquired trademark rights from Sanfilippo. In March 2024, Redis Ltd. relicensed version 7.4+ under RSAL/SSPL (source-available, non-free), prompting the Linux Foundation to fork the last BSD-licensed version as Valkey.
Redis.summary_abuse: Redis is an in-memory database engine with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. It is a backend infrastructure component.

Redis Object Cache.history: Redis Object Cache is a WordPress plugin by Till Kruss (Germany) that integrates WordPress with a Redis backend to cache database objects, first released around 2015. It is one of the most widely used WordPress caching plugins and is listed on WordPress.org. A Pro version with additional features has been offered. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Redis Object Cache.summary_abuse: Redis Object Cache is a backend WordPress caching plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is a server-side performance tool with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Reditus.history: Reditus was founded in 2020 by Joran Hofman in the Netherlands as a partner and affiliate management platform built specifically for B2B SaaS companies. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events since launch.
Reditus.summary_abuse: Reditus is a B2B SaaS affiliate management tool with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by software companies to manage partner and affiliate programs, with no consumer-facing surface area that fraudsters would target.

Redmine.history: Redmine was created by Jean-Philippe Lang and first released in 2006 as a free and open-source project management and issue tracking system built on Ruby on Rails. It was inspired by Basecamp and Trac. It has been continuously maintained by an independent community of contributors and remains one of the most widely deployed self-hosted project management tools.
Redmine.summary_abuse: Redmine is a self-hosted open-source project management tool with no notable abuse signal. It has no consumer-facing hosted offering and no online service that fraudsters would target.

ReDoc.history: ReDoc was created as an open-source OpenAPI documentation renderer, originally hosted under Rebilly on GitHub before being moved to the Redocly organization. Redocly has built a commercial platform (Redocly) around the open-source ReDoc CE core, offering a dual-license model.
ReDoc.summary_abuse: ReDoc is an open-source API documentation renderer with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool for rendering OpenAPI specs and has no consumer-facing surface exploitable by fraudsters.

Redonner.history: Redonner is a French platform that incentivises textile donation and recycling by rewarding consumers for donating used clothing.
Redonner.summary_abuse: Redonner has no notable abuse signal. It is a sustainability rewards platform for textile recycling with no known fraud exposure.

Redrive.history: Redrive is a Brazilian WhatsApp sales automation platform helping companies communicate with customers and automate sales workflows.
Redrive.summary_abuse: Redrive has no notable abuse signal beyond the general risk that WhatsApp automation tools could be used for spam. No documented large-scale abuse is known.

RedShop.history: RedShop is a Joomla-based e-commerce extension from Denmark, providing SMEs with an open-source shopping platform. It has been active since the mid-2000s.
RedShop.summary_abuse: RedShop is an open-source Joomla e-commerce extension with no notable abuse signal.

Redux.history: Redux was created by Dan Abramov and Andrew Clark in 2015, inspired by Flux and Elm. It quickly became the de-facto state management solution for React applications and remains one of the most downloaded JavaScript libraries.
Redux.summary_abuse: Redux is a foundational JavaScript state management library with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Redux Framework.history: Redux Framework was first released in December 2012 as an extensible options framework for WordPress themes and plugins, based on the NHP Theme Options Framework. It quickly became one of the most widely adopted options frameworks in the WordPress ecosystem. Redux is 100% open source and continues to receive active updates as of 2026.
Redux Framework.summary_abuse: Redux Framework is a WordPress developer library for building settings panels. It has no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

RedwoodJS.history: RedwoodJS is a full-stack serverless JavaScript framework created by Tom Preston-Werner (GitHub co-founder) and contributors, first released in 2020. It combines React, GraphQL, Prisma, and deployment to serverless functions into an opinionated full-stack framework.
RedwoodJS.summary_abuse: RedwoodJS is an open-source full-stack web framework with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Reelevant.history: Reelevant was founded around 2016 in France as a visual content personalization platform, helping brands like Carrefour, L'Oreal, and Decathlon deliver on-demand, data-driven visual content. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are known.
Reelevant.summary_abuse: Reelevant is a B2B personalization platform focused on marketing content delivery for enterprise brands. It has no notable abuse signal as it is a closed SaaS tool used by vetted enterprise clients, with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Reevoo.history: Reevoo was founded in 2005 in the UK by Ben Griffiths, Richard Anson, and Guy Logan as a user-generated review and CX insights platform. In August 2021, Feefo, a competing reviews and CX insights company, acquired Reevoo to expand its global reach and capabilities.
Reevoo.summary_abuse: Reevoo is a B2B reviews and UGC platform serving brand clients. There is no notable abuse signal; the platform is used by verified enterprise clients for collecting legitimate customer reviews, with no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudsters.

Refari.history: Refari is an Australian digital recruitment platform providing candidate engagement, job board, and referral tools for staffing agencies.
Refari.summary_abuse: Refari has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B recruitment tools platform with no consumer-facing free hosting surface.

Refericon.history: Refericon was founded in 2017 in Poznan, Poland, as a cloud-based referral marketing platform targeting e-commerce businesses. No notable ownership changes or events are known.
Refericon.summary_abuse: Refericon is a niche referral marketing SaaS. Referral programs can occasionally be abused by users attempting to game reward systems, but this is an issue for the businesses using the platform rather than a systemic platform abuse concern. No notable fraud or phishing incidents involving Refericon are documented.

Referral Rocket.history: Referral Rocket is a referral and affiliate marketing software solution for businesses seeking to run structured referral programs.
Referral Rocket.summary_abuse: Referral Rocket has no notable abuse signal. It is a referral marketing tool and not a consumer-facing platform fraudsters exploit.

ReferralCandy.history: ReferralCandy was founded in 2009 in Singapore by Dinesh Raju and Zach Cheng, both Carnegie Mellon alumni, as a customer referral platform for e-commerce. The platform has grown to serve thousands of online stores globally with no major acquisitions or ownership changes.
ReferralCandy.summary_abuse: ReferralCandy is a referral marketing platform primarily used by e-commerce brands. Referral programs can be gamed by users creating fake referrals or duplicate accounts, but these risks are managed at the merchant level. No systemic abuse or fraud incidents are documented involving the platform itself.

Refersion.history: Refersion was founded in 2014 as an affiliate and influencer management platform. In July 2020, it was acquired by Assembly, a digital marketing and media company.
Refersion.summary_abuse: Refersion is an affiliate tracking and management platform. Affiliate programs can be misused to route fraudulent referrals or generate fake traffic, but this is an industry-wide issue and Refersion includes tracking controls to mitigate it. No specific widespread abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

Refiner.history: Refiner is a German SaaS customer survey tool designed specifically for SaaS companies to run in-app surveys with high response rates.
Refiner.summary_abuse: Refiner has no notable abuse signal. It is a SaaS survey tool with no consumer-facing free hosting.

Refix.history: Refix is a website analytics and user engagement tracking tool monitoring clicks, scrolls, and other behavioural events.
Refix.summary_abuse: Refix has no notable abuse signal. It is a site analytics tool with no consumer-facing hosting surface.

Reflektion.history: Reflektion was founded in 2012 by Amar Chokhawala, a former Google engineer, as an AI-powered e-commerce personalization platform. In September 2021, Sitecore acquired Reflektion to strengthen its AI-driven digital experience capabilities.
Reflektion.summary_abuse: Reflektion is a B2B e-commerce personalization platform integrated directly into enterprise retailer infrastructure. It has no notable abuse signal as it operates as a backend service for vetted enterprise clients with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Reflex.history: Reflex launched in December 2022 as Pynecone, an open-source framework for building full-stack web apps in pure Python. It was renamed Reflex in early 2023. The project is backed by Y Combinator and maintained by a small startup team led by Alek Petuskey and Nikhil Rao.
Reflex.summary_abuse: Reflex is an open-source Python web framework. It has no notable abuse signal; it is a developer tool used to build web applications and carries no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Reform.history: Reform was founded in 2021 and is incorporated in the United States. The company provides a no-code form builder focused on high-converting lead generation with features like conditional logic and multi-step forms. It is a small startup with no notable acquisitions or ownership changes recorded.
Reform.summary_abuse: Reform is a form builder SaaS with a free tier. Form builders carry a moderate risk of being used to harvest credentials or personal data via phishing forms, but no specific documented incidents involving Reform have been reported. Its subscription-based model requires an account, which provides some barrier against purely anonymous misuse.

Refundid.history: Refundid was founded in July 2020 in Australia, launching commercially in February 2021, as a fintech providing instant refunds for e-commerce returns. The company raised $3 million in 2021 and expanded to the US market.
Refundid.summary_abuse: Refundid is a fintech return-refund platform. Instant refund services carry inherent fraud risk from customers filing false return claims or abusing refund policies, which is a known issue in the e-commerce returns space. The platform works directly with merchants, and abuse risk is primarily borne by the merchants rather than by Refundid's infrastructure itself.

REG.RU.history: REG.RU was founded in 2006 by Alexey Korolyuk as a domain registrar and hosting provider. It became Russia's largest domain registrar, managing over 1.9 million domains, and in 2012 became an ICANN-accredited registrar.
REG.RU.summary_abuse: REG.RU, as one of Russia's largest domain registrars and hosting providers, has been associated with hosting abuse including malware distribution, phishing campaigns, and spam operations. Russian hosting providers operating outside Western legal frameworks have historically faced slower abuse response times. The .ru TLD and providers like REG.RU are frequently cited in threat intelligence reports for hosting cybercriminal infrastructure.

Regiondo.history: Regiondo was founded in 2011 in Munich, Germany, by Oliver Nutzel and Yann Maurer. It initially operated as a consumer marketplace for tours and activities before pivoting to a B2B booking software for tour operators and activity providers around 2015. The platform now serves over 7,500 suppliers in 42 countries.
Regiondo.summary_abuse: Regiondo is a B2B booking management platform for tour operators. It has no notable abuse signal as it serves verified business clients in the tourism sector with no consumer-facing infrastructure that fraudsters would target.

Registria.history: Registria was founded around 2012 in the US as a post-purchase product registration and customer engagement platform serving durable goods companies. The company has received venture funding and expanded to global enterprise clients.
Registria.summary_abuse: Registria is a B2B product registration platform. It collects consumer data on behalf of durable goods brands but has no notable abuse signal as it operates as a backend service under direct enterprise contracts.

Regula.history: Regula was founded in 1992 in Daugavpils, Latvia, developing forensic document verification devices and software. Over three decades it expanded into identity verification SaaS solutions serving governments, banks, and enterprise clients globally. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are known.
Regula.summary_abuse: Regula provides identity verification and document authentication technology. The platform is used to detect fraud rather than facilitate it. No notable abuse signal; it serves as a defensive security layer for enterprise clients with no consumer-facing surface for fraudsters to target.

REI Chat.history: REI Chat is a small live chat solution with limited public documentation available.
REI Chat.summary_abuse: REI Chat has no notable abuse signal based on available information. It appears to be a small live chat widget with no known consumer-facing hosting surface that fraudsters target.

Reinvigorate.history: Reinvigorate was founded in 2002 as a real-time web analytics and heatmaps service, entering a partnership with Media Temple in 2005. In August 2011 it was acquired by Webtrends, rendering it largely obsolete as a standalone product. The service is now effectively deprecated.
Reinvigorate.summary_abuse: Reinvigorate was a web analytics tracker. As a now-deprecated service absorbed into Webtrends, it has no ongoing notable abuse signal. When active, analytics scripts can be abused to fingerprint users, but no significant documented incidents were specific to Reinvigorate.

Rela.history: Rela is an Australian real estate marketing platform providing digital tools for property professionals to create and distribute listings.
Rela.summary_abuse: Rela has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B real estate marketing platform with no consumer-facing free hosting surface.

Relais Colis.history: Relais Colis is a French parcel delivery network serving the French e-commerce market through a network of pickup points. It is a well-established national carrier in France. No notable ownership changes or major events are publicly documented.
Relais Colis.summary_abuse: Relais Colis is a French parcel delivery carrier. Shipping carriers can be impersonated in phishing scams (fake delivery notifications) but this risk applies to the Relais Colis brand rather than the platform itself. No notable abuse incidents involving the Relais Colis tech stack specifically are documented.

Relap.history: Relap is a Russian content recommendation and advertising effectiveness platform optimising editorial content discovery.
Relap.summary_abuse: Relap is a Russian-based advertising platform. Its data handling practices are not subject to GDPR and content recommendations could include low-quality ad traffic patterns, but no specific documented fraud campaigns are known.

Released.history: Released (released.so) is a product changelog and release notes SaaS tool that integrates deeply with Jira to auto-generate release notes from tickets using AI. It offers embeddable announcement widgets and a free tier for up to 10 users. The founding year and headquarters are not publicly disclosed.
Released.summary_abuse: Released is a product changelog and release notes tool with no notable abuse signal. Its B2B developer tooling focus and Jira integration requirement make it unsuitable as a vehicle for phishing or consumer-facing fraud.

Releasit COD Form & Upsells.history: Releasit COD Form & Upsells is a Shopify app replacing the standard checkout with a cash-on-delivery form and upsell flows, popular with dropshipping merchants.
Releasit COD Form & Upsells.summary_abuse: Releasit is commonly associated with COD dropshipping operations which carry elevated fraud risk. Cash-on-delivery dropshipping storefronts frequently appear in scam and counterfeit goods complaints, and tools enabling this checkout pattern can facilitate those storefronts.

Releva.history: Releva is a Bulgarian AI-powered marketing automation and revenue management platform for ecommerce.
Releva.summary_abuse: Releva has no notable abuse signal. It is a marketing automation platform for ecommerce with no consumer-facing free hosting surface.

Relewise.history: Relewise was founded in 2020 in Aarhus, Denmark, by Brian Holmgard Kristensen, Sune Hansen, and Ronnie Balle as an AI-powered e-commerce personalization and search engine. The company raised an 800,000 EUR pre-seed round.
Relewise.summary_abuse: Relewise is a B2B e-commerce personalization engine. It has no notable abuse signal as it operates as a backend service under direct enterprise contracts with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

RELOOP.history: RELOOP is a peer-to-peer clothing resale platform enabling users to buy and sell clothing through personal networks.
RELOOP.summary_abuse: RELOOP is a peer-to-peer clothing resale marketplace with limited scale. Like other C2C marketplaces it has potential for counterfeit goods or scam listings, but no documented large-scale abuse patterns are known.

Remarkable Commerce.history: Remarkable Commerce is a UK-based e-commerce platform provider serving mid-sized retailers. No detailed founding history or notable ownership events are publicly documented.
Remarkable Commerce.summary_abuse: Remarkable Commerce is a B2B e-commerce platform for mid-sized retailers. It has no notable abuse signal as it is a closed platform serving vetted retail clients with no anonymous consumer-facing surface.

Remedo.history: Remedo was founded in 2016 in India as a digital healthcare marketing platform helping doctors and clinics improve their online presence through SEO, ads, and AI-driven tools. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are known.
