RevJet.history: RevJet is an ad experience platform for enterprise advertisers, providing cross-channel ad management and creative optimization. No notable ownership changes are publicly documented.
RevJet.summary_abuse: RevJet is an enterprise ad platform with minimal direct abuse risk. As with all programmatic advertising infrastructure, it participates in the behavioral tracking ecosystem, but it operates in a B2B enterprise context with no documented specific abuse incidents.

RevLifter.history: RevLifter is a UK-based AI coupon and personalized incentives platform founded around 2017. It helps brands offer contextual discounts and vouchers to shoppers. No notable ownership changes are documented.
RevLifter.summary_abuse: RevLifter is a coupon and incentives platform. Coupon technologies can occasionally be used to generate unauthorized discount codes or enable fraudulent coupon stacking, but RevLifter operates with merchant authorization controls. No specific abuse incidents are documented.

RevolverMaps.history: RevolverMaps was a free 3D visitor map widget service launched around 2009, popular with bloggers for displaying real-time visitor locations via IP geolocation. The service has reportedly shut down.
RevolverMaps.summary_abuse: RevolverMaps is a visitor tracking widget that has reportedly shut down. When active, it collected IP-based geolocation data without explicit user consent, raising GDPR concerns. No major fraud or malware incidents are documented.

RevTrax.history: RevTrax is a US offer management and coupon tracking platform founded around 2005. It helps brands track digital coupon performance and connect offers to in-store sales. No notable ownership changes are documented.
RevTrax.summary_abuse: RevTrax is a coupon and offer management platform with low abuse risk. Digital coupon platforms can be used for coupon fraud, but RevTrax operates with brand-authorized offers and tracking. No specific fraud incidents are documented.

Revv.history: Revv is a US-based fundraising and lead optimization platform, particularly popular with political campaigns and nonprofits. It enables online donations and donor acquisition.
Revv.summary_abuse: Revv is a fundraising and donation platform with minor abuse potential. Political fundraising platforms can be exploited for fraudulent donation solicitation, but Revv is a legitimate platform serving verified political and nonprofit campaigns with no documented significant abuse history.

Revy.history: Revy is a Shopify apps builder focused on creating revenue-generating apps for merchants. It is a small startup with limited public history.
Revy.summary_abuse: Revy is a Shopify app development platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Rewardful.history: Rewardful was founded around 2018 as a bootstrapped affiliate and referral program management tool designed specifically for SaaS companies using Stripe. It was acquired by Paddle in 2022, adding native support for Paddle-billed subscriptions.
Rewardful.summary_abuse: Rewardful is an affiliate management platform for SaaS companies. Like all affiliate platforms, it can be subject to fraudulent referrals, but the vendor includes tracking safeguards and the product is B2B-focused with no consumer-facing content hosting that would attract phishing actors.

Rewix.history: Rewix is a B2B ecommerce platform developed by Italian company Zero11, founded around 2010 in Italy. The platform is built on a Java stack and focuses on enterprise-scale B2B commerce; no notable acquisitions or rebranding events are documented.
Rewix.summary_abuse: Rewix is a B2B ecommerce platform targeting enterprise clients and has no notable abuse signal. It serves authenticated business customers and does not offer anonymous public-facing sign-up or consumer-level free tiers, making it an unlikely target for phishing campaigns or fraudulent storefronts.

Rezdy.history: Rezdy was founded in 2011 in Sydney, Australia by Simon Lenoir as an online booking platform for the tours and activities industry. In 2023 it was acquired by an undisclosed US private equity firm for over $100 million in a deal that also brought together Checkfront and Regiondo into a consolidated platform group.
Rezdy.summary_abuse: Rezdy is a booking software platform for tours and attractions with no notable abuse signal. The platform is merchant-facing rather than consumer-facing for hosting arbitrary content, and there are no documented instances of it being used for phishing, fraud, or scam operations.

Rezgo.history: Rezgo was founded in 2005 in North Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada by June Ohashi and Stephen Joyce as subscription-free booking software for tour and activity operators. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are recorded.
Rezgo.summary_abuse: Rezgo is a booking platform for tour operators with no notable abuse signal. It serves business operators managing bookings rather than hosting arbitrary consumer content, and no documented phishing or fraud incidents are associated with the platform.

Rezku.history: Rezku was founded in 2012 by Paul Katsch as a restaurant POS system and is a product of Guest Innovations, headquartered in Sacramento, California. No notable acquisitions or rebranding events are documented.
Rezku.summary_abuse: Rezku is a restaurant POS and management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a closed business-to-business tool for restaurant operators and does not provide a consumer-facing hosting surface that would attract phishing campaigns or fraudulent storefronts.

RhinoFit.history: RhinoFit was founded in 2012 in Florida by Adam Morden as a cloud-based gym management software for fitness facilities and CrossFit-style affiliate gyms. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are recorded.
RhinoFit.summary_abuse: RhinoFit is a gym management SaaS platform with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a closed business tool for fitness facility management and does not expose consumer-facing hosting surfaces suitable for phishing or fraud.

Rhymix.history: Rhymix is an open-source PHP CMS forked from XpressEngine 1.x (originally created by NAVER in the early 2000s as ZeroBoard XE) by the Korean user community around 2014. It reverted the license from LGPL back to GPL to strengthen free-software protections, distinguishing it from NAVER's commercial XpressEngine lineage.
Rhymix.summary_abuse: Rhymix is a self-hosted open-source CMS primarily used by Korean websites. Because it is self-hosted, abuse risk depends on the operator rather than a central vendor. No specific abuse campaigns or vulnerabilities attributable to Rhymix itself are prominently documented, though any self-hosted CMS can be misconfigured or left unpatched.

Rich Plugins Reviews.history: Rich Plugins Reviews is a WordPress plugin for displaying verified reviews from Google, Facebook, and other review sources. It has been in the WordPress plugin ecosystem for several years.
Rich Plugins Reviews.summary_abuse: Rich Plugins Reviews is a WordPress review display plugin with no notable abuse signal. It aggregates verified third-party reviews and has no documented history of exploitation.

Richpanel.history: Richpanel was founded around 2018 and raised seed funding from Sequoia's Surge accelerator. The company focuses on CRM and helpdesk software tailored for DTC and ecommerce brands.
Richpanel.summary_abuse: Richpanel is a B2B customer support and CRM platform for ecommerce merchants with no notable abuse signal. It is a closed business tool that does not provide consumer-facing hosting or anonymous public access.

RichRelevance.history: RichRelevance was founded in 2007 as an omnichannel personalization and product recommendation platform. It serves large retailers with AI-driven personalization. No notable ownership changes are publicly documented.
RichRelevance.summary_abuse: RichRelevance is an enterprise ecommerce personalization platform. It collects behavioral data for product recommendations and personalized shopping experiences, placing it in the ad-tech adjacent category. Its primary use is legitimate B2B retail personalization with no documented fraud abuse.

Rickshaw.history: Rickshaw is a JavaScript toolkit for creating interactive time series graphs, built by Shutterstock and open-sourced around 2012. It is built on top of D3.js. Activity has significantly declined as more modern charting libraries emerged.
Rickshaw.summary_abuse: Rickshaw is a JavaScript charting library with no notable abuse signal. It is a data visualization library with no consumer-facing surface that fraud actors would target.

Riddle.history: Riddle Technologies AG was founded in 2014 in Germany as an interactive content platform for creating quizzes, polls, and personality tests. The company acquired competitor qzzr.com to expand its customer base. Clients include the BBC, CNN, and Shopify.
Riddle.summary_abuse: Riddle is a quiz and poll creation platform with a modest abuse risk. Because it allows users to embed interactive widgets on any site and offers a free tier, it could theoretically be used to create deceptive quiz-style phishing lures, but no documented campaign specifically targeting the platform is known. The company is GDPR-compliant and EU-based.

RideBits.history: RideBits is a SaaS booking and trip management platform for ground transportation companies. It is a niche transportation tech product with limited public history.
RideBits.summary_abuse: RideBits is a transportation booking SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B product for transportation operators with no documented history of exploitation.

RightJS.history: RightJS was a modular JavaScript framework created around 2008 as an alternative to jQuery. It has been effectively abandoned as jQuery and modern frameworks dominated the market. It remains in legacy codebases but has no active development.
RightJS.summary_abuse: RightJS is an abandoned JavaScript framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a legacy frontend library with no consumer-facing surface that fraud actors would target.

RightMessage.history: RightMessage was founded in 2017 by Brennan Dunn and Shai Schechter as a website and email personalization platform. It is a bootstrapped indie SaaS aimed at small-business marketers.
RightMessage.summary_abuse: RightMessage is a website personalization tool that injects content variants based on visitor segments. No specific abuse history is documented. As a content personalization layer it could theoretically be misused for targeted deception, but no documented incidents are known and legitimate use dominates.

Ringba.history: Ringba is a US call tracking and pay-per-call advertising platform founded around 2013. It serves affiliate marketers and performance advertisers running call-based campaigns. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Ringba.summary_abuse: Ringba is a pay-per-call and call tracking platform used in performance marketing. The pay-per-call advertising industry has documented cases of call fraud, including incentivized or fraudulent calls to drive per-call payouts. While Ringba has fraud detection tools, the pay-per-call affiliate ecosystem inherently carries elevated fraud risk.

RingCaptcha.history: RingCaptcha is a phone verification and user onboarding service providing SMS OTP authentication for apps. No notable ownership changes are documented.
RingCaptcha.summary_abuse: RingCaptcha is a phone verification and SMS OTP service. SMS-based verification services are occasionally abused for SMS flooding or OTP interception attacks, but RingCaptcha's primary use is legitimate user verification. No specific documented abuse incidents are reported.

Ringostat.history: Ringostat is a Ukrainian AI-powered call tracking and business phone system platform founded around 2013. It provides marketing attribution through call tracking analytics. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Ringostat.summary_abuse: Ringostat is a call tracking and phone analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B marketing analytics tool used by businesses for call attribution and has no consumer-facing features exploited by fraud actors.

Rinsed.history: Rinsed was founded in 2019 by Austin Esecson, Sam Logan, and Nic Hippenmeyer via Y Combinator and subsequently raised $12M in Series A (2022) from Bedrock Capital and Founders Fund, followed by $20M in Series B (2023) from VMG Technology.
Rinsed.summary_abuse: Rinsed is a vertical CRM for the car wash industry with no notable abuse signal. It targets business operators in a specialized vertical and does not expose consumer-facing public hosting surfaces.

RioSEO.history: Rio SEO was spun out as its own brand by Covario, Inc. in April 2012 as a local search and SEO automation platform. In July 2022 it was acquired by Forsta, a customer experience and market research software company.
RioSEO.summary_abuse: RioSEO is a local search marketing management platform serving enterprise brands and has no notable abuse signal. It is a closed B2B tool focused on organic search and does not provide consumer-facing hosting surfaces.

Riot.history: Riot.js was created in 2013 by Muut (a forum/comment platform) as a minimalistic component-based UI library. It was designed as a lightweight alternative to larger frameworks, featuring Web Components-like custom tags with a tiny footprint. No major ownership changes or deprecation events since.
Riot.summary_abuse: Riot.js is a frontend JavaScript library with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend rendering tool used by developers with no consumer-facing hosted content or mechanisms that would be useful for phishing or fraud.

Riot.js.history: Riot.js was developed in 2013 by Muut as a minimalistic component-based UI library for building web interfaces. It features custom HTML tags with a tiny footprint (~6KB) and was positioned as a simpler alternative to Angular and React. No corporate acquisition or notable governance changes have occurred.
Riot.js.summary_abuse: Riot.js is an open-source JavaScript UI library with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. It is used by developers to build web applications and has no characteristics that would attract fraudulent use.

Ripple.history: Ripple is the frontend framework for Victoria's Single Digital Presence (SDP), an Australian government initiative to unify state government digital services using Nuxt and Vue.js. It was developed by the Department of Premier and Cabinet in Victoria, Australia.
Ripple.summary_abuse: Ripple is a government-sector OSS frontend framework with no notable abuse signal. It has no consumer-facing hosted content and is used exclusively by Australian government departments.

Rise.ai.history: Rise.ai was founded in 2015 in Tel Aviv by Yair Miron as a gift card and store credit platform for ecommerce brands. In May 2021 it was acquired by Wix.com to extend Wix's ecommerce platform with loyalty and gift card capabilities.
Rise.ai.summary_abuse: Rise.ai is a gift card and loyalty platform for ecommerce merchants with low documented abuse. Gift card platforms can be targeted by fraudsters reselling stolen gift card balances, but Rise.ai operates as a B2B tool for merchants rather than a consumer redemption network, limiting direct consumer-facing abuse surface.

Riskified.history: Riskified was founded in 2012 in Tel Aviv by Eido Gal and Assaf Feldman as an ecommerce fraud prevention platform. The company raised $232M in funding and went public on the NYSE in July 2021 at a $4.3 billion valuation.
Riskified.summary_abuse: Riskified is a fraud prevention platform with no notable abuse signal on the infrastructure side. Its purpose is anti-fraud and chargeback prevention for merchants, and as a publicly traded company it operates under tight compliance and governance standards.

Rispose.history: Rispose is a recent AI agent and chatbot SaaS for automating customer support and sales. It is a post-2022 product with limited public history.
Rispose.summary_abuse: Rispose is an AI agent platform with no notable abuse signal. AI chatbot platforms can theoretically be misused for social engineering, but Rispose has no documented history of exploitation.

RiteCMS.history: RiteCMS is a fork of PhpSqliteCMS, an ultrafast lightweight PHP/SQLite CMS. It has been maintained on GitHub since around 2012 with version 3.0 releasing in 2021 introducing major architectural changes.
RiteCMS.summary_abuse: RiteCMS is an open-source PHP CMS with some abuse potential. Its CPE in security databases indicates documented vulnerabilities have been tracked against it. Like other self-hosted CMS platforms, outdated or unpatched installations could be exploited, but the project itself is not used as infrastructure for scams or fraud.

RiteKit.history: RiteKit was founded in 2012 by Saul Fleischman as a social media optimization platform built around hashtag generation and social sharing tools. It remains a small independent venture.
RiteKit.summary_abuse: RiteKit is a social media hashtag and automation tool with no notable abuse signal for fraud or phishing. The API is publicly accessible and could theoretically be used for spam hashtag injection, but no documented campaigns are associated with it.

Rive.history: Rive was founded in 2016 in San Francisco as a real-time interactive animation tool originally known as Flare. The company raised a $4M seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz in 2019 and a $10M Series A led by Two Sigma Ventures in January 2023.
Rive.summary_abuse: Rive is an animation and interactive design tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer-focused design platform with no consumer-facing hosting that would attract phishing or fraud activity.

riyo.ai.history: riyo.ai (formerly Traek) is an India-based behavioral analytics and lead generation SaaS platform. It identifies website visitors and provides session replay, heatmaps, and lead generation features. Founded around 2020 in Ahmedabad, Gujarat.
riyo.ai.summary_abuse: riyo.ai is a behavioral analytics tool that tracks website visitor behavior in detail, including session replays and visitor identification. It collects substantial personal data about website visitors which raises privacy concerns. The platform targets marketers and sales teams for lead generation rather than facilitating direct fraud.

RMZ.history: RMZ is a freemium ecommerce store creation platform available at rmz.gg. It appears to be a small, recent product aimed at users wanting to create online stores quickly.
RMZ.summary_abuse: RMZ is a free-tier ecommerce store builder with modest abuse potential. Free ecommerce platforms can be used for fake or scam storefronts, but there is no specific documented abuse history for RMZ.

RND.history: RND is a niche ecommerce infrastructure SaaS with limited public documentation available.
RND.summary_abuse: RND has no notable abuse signal based on available information.

Roadiz CMS.history: Roadiz CMS is an open-source polymorphic CMS built on the Symfony ecosystem (Symfony Flex, Doctrine ORM, API Platform, Twig), developed primarily by a French development team. It is designed for API-first and headless CMS architectures. No major governance changes or acquisitions documented.
Roadiz CMS.summary_abuse: Roadiz CMS is a self-hosted PHP CMS with no notable abuse signal. As with any self-hosted CMS, compromised or misconfigured installations could serve malicious content, but this is not specific to Roadiz.

RoadRunner.history: RoadRunner was developed starting in 2017 by Anton Titov at Spiral Scout to handle peak loads for large PHP applications, and was open-sourced in 2018 under MIT license. It serves as a high-performance PHP application server written in Go, replacing traditional PHP-FPM setups. The project moved to the roadrunner-server GitHub organization for ongoing development.
RoadRunner.summary_abuse: RoadRunner is a server-side PHP runtime with no notable abuse signal. It runs as an application server infrastructure component with no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudulent use.

Roas Funnels.history: Roas Funnels is an all-in-one digital marketing and funnel-building platform. It is a small, recent startup with limited public history.
Roas Funnels.summary_abuse: Roas Funnels is a marketing automation and funnel platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a small B2B marketing tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Robarov.history: Robarov is a Belgian online marketing platform focused on local digital marketing strategies. Very limited public information is available about its founding or history.
Robarov.summary_abuse: Robarov is a small regional online marketing platform with no documented abuse history. Limited public information makes a comprehensive assessment difficult.

Robin.history: Robin was founded in 2014 in Boston, Massachusetts as a hybrid workplace management platform for desk booking and room scheduling. The company raised $20M in 2021 and $30M in July 2022 to expand its office reservation software as hybrid work became mainstream.
Robin.summary_abuse: Robin is a workplace management SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It serves corporate customers managing office space and does not expose consumer-facing hosting or anonymous public access.

RobixCM DV Team.history: RobixCM DV Team is an Iranian web design and management service operating at robixcm.ir. It is a small Iranian web agency with very limited international visibility.
RobixCM DV Team.summary_abuse: RobixCM DV Team is an Iranian web services provider with limited public information. The Iranian origin means verifiability of security practices is limited. No specific documented abuse incidents are known.

RobixCM Generator.history: RobixCM Generator is an Iranian web design and website generation service, closely related to RobixCM DV Team from the same operator at robixcm.ir.
RobixCM Generator.summary_abuse: RobixCM Generator is an Iranian web services product with limited public information. The Iranian origin limits verifiability of practices. No specific documented abuse incidents are known.

Robly.history: Robly was founded around 2013 in New York City as an email marketing platform with a focus on improving open rates via its OpenGen technology. It targets non-profits, charities, and religious organizations.
Robly.summary_abuse: Robly is an email marketing platform with the standard spam and phishing abuse risk inherent to email senders. Like all bulk email platforms, it can be misused to send spam or phishing messages if account verification controls are insufficient. No specific public incidents distinguish Robly from other small email marketing providers.

Robofy.history: Robofy is a post-2022 AI chatbot platform enabling businesses to create custom chatbots trained on their own website content using ChatGPT-style models. Limited founding history is publicly available.
Robofy.summary_abuse: Robofy is an AI chatbot creation platform with a modest abuse risk. As a wrapper around large language model APIs, it could be misused to create convincing phishing chatbots or social engineering bots, a risk common to all low-code chatbot platforms. No specific documented incidents are associated with Robofy.

RoboReception.history: RoboReception is a UK-based digital receptionist and chatbot platform aimed at healthcare and clinical settings to manage patient inquiries and reduce front-desk workload. Limited public history is available.
RoboReception.summary_abuse: RoboReception is a niche healthcare chatbot tool with no documented abuse history. It is a B2B product for clinical practices with no consumer-facing public hosting surface.

ROC Commerce.history: ROC Commerce (Real Omni-Channel Commerce) was launched in 2014 as a dedicated B2B and B2C omnichannel ecommerce platform by Americaneagle.com, a family-owned web development agency. Earlier ecommerce tools under Americaneagle.com predated this rebranding.
ROC Commerce.summary_abuse: ROC Commerce has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B/B2C ecommerce platform sold to enterprise clients, with no consumer-facing free tier that fraudsters would target.

Rock Content.history: Rock Content was founded in 2013 in Brazil as a content marketing platform. The company raised $30M in Series B funding in 2021 from Unbox Capital and other investors to expand globally. It remains Brazil-headquartered with significant Latin American market presence.
Rock Content.summary_abuse: Rock Content is a content marketing platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves marketing teams and agencies with content workflow tools rather than providing hosting surfaces commonly exploited for phishing or fraud.

Rockee.history: Rockee is a content feedback and analytics SaaS providing insight into how audiences perceive published content. It is a small, recent product.
Rockee.summary_abuse: Rockee is a content feedback platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B analytics tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Rockerbox.history: Rockerbox was founded in 2013 as a multi-touch marketing attribution platform. It raised $540K in seed funding and grew organically before being acquired by DoubleVerify in February 2025 for $85 million.
Rockerbox.summary_abuse: Rockerbox is a marketing attribution and analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a closed B2B measurement tool that does not provide consumer-facing hosting, making it an unlikely target for phishing or fraud operations.

Rocket Tools.history: Rocket Tools is an AI marketing analytics platform focused on shopping mall retail data, connecting brands to mall visitor insights. Very limited public history is available.
Rocket Tools.summary_abuse: Rocket Tools is a niche retail analytics platform with no documented abuse history. It serves marketing teams with mall data insights and does not expose a consumer-facing hosting surface.

Rocket.Chat.history: Rocket.Chat was created in 2015 by Gabriel Engel in Porto Alegre, Brazil as an open-source Slack alternative for enterprise communication. The company raised $37M across multiple rounds, with investors including NEA, Valor Capital Group, and Greycroft. It has grown to become one of the most prominent open-source team messaging platforms, particularly popular for self-hosted enterprise deployments.
Rocket.Chat.summary_abuse: Rocket.Chat is a legitimate open-source communication platform with no notable dedicated abuse signal. Like any self-hosted or SaaS messaging platform, it could theoretically be used for criminal coordination, but this is not a documented pattern specific to Rocket.Chat. Its self-hosted option and privacy focus make it popular with security-conscious organizations.

Rocketfy.history: Rocketfy is a Mexican ecommerce and dropshipping platform aimed at Latin American entrepreneurs. Limited founding history is publicly available.
Rocketfy.summary_abuse: Rocketfy is a dropshipping and online store creation platform targeting the Latin American market. Dropshipping platforms carry a moderate abuse risk as they can be used to create counterfeit or fraudulent storefronts. No specific documented incidents are associated with Rocketfy.

Rocketspark.history: Rocketspark was founded in 2009 in Cambridge, New Zealand as a website builder with integrated ecommerce for small businesses. It has remained independently owned with a focus on the New Zealand and Australian markets.
Rocketspark.summary_abuse: Rocketspark is a small-business website builder primarily serving the New Zealand market with a modest abuse risk profile. As a hosted website builder it could be used to create fraudulent storefronts, but no documented abuse campaigns are associated with the platform and its limited scale reduces attractiveness to bulk fraudsters.

RockRMS.history: Rock RMS was developed by the Spark Development Network, a nonprofit organization, and released around 2014 as an open-source CMS and relationship management system specifically designed for churches. It has grown to become a prominent free church management platform and continues active development by the nonprofit.
RockRMS.summary_abuse: Rock RMS is a self-hosted church CRM and CMS with no notable abuse signal. As a niche platform for religious organizations, it has no consumer-facing content hosting or characteristics that attract fraudulent use.

Rofofk.history: Rofofk is an Arabic-language multi-tenant ecommerce SaaS platform serving markets in the Middle East and North Africa. Limited public founding history is available.
Rofofk.summary_abuse: Rofofk is a niche Arabic ecommerce platform serving MENA merchants. As a hosted storefront builder it carries the standard risk of fake storefronts, but no specific documented abuse incidents are publicly associated with the platform.

Roistat.history: Roistat was founded in 2014 in Moscow, Russia by German Gavrilov and Evgeniy Udodov as a marketing analytics and call-tracking system. It primarily serves Russian-speaking markets.
Roistat.summary_abuse: Roistat is a marketing analytics platform with no notable abuse signal specific to phishing or fraud. It is a B2B analytics tool primarily used in Russian-speaking markets; no documented abuse campaigns are associated with the platform.

Rokt.history: Rokt was founded in 2012 in Sydney, Australia by Bruce Buchanan. It built an AI-driven ecommerce transaction marketing platform and raised over $800M in total funding, reaching a $3.5B valuation in 2025. The company is positioning for a potential Nasdaq IPO.
Rokt.summary_abuse: Rokt is an ecommerce transaction marketing platform that injects personalized offers during checkout flows. Because it presents third-party offers to consumers during purchases, there is a risk of misleading or dark-pattern adjacent upsells, though it is not a phishing or fraud tool. The company operates at significant scale and works with major enterprise retailers.

Rollbar.history: Rollbar was founded in 2012 by Brian Rue and Cory Virok in San Francisco under the name Ratchet.io before rebranding to Rollbar. It raised $17.4M over 4 rounds including a Series B in 2020 and has grown to serve thousands of development teams with real-time error tracking.
Rollbar.summary_abuse: Rollbar is an error monitoring and logging platform used by developer and operations teams. It has no notable abuse signal; it is a B2B backend tool with no consumer-facing content hosting surface that would be exploited by phishing actors or scammers.

Roller.history: ROLLER was founded in 2010 in Melbourne, Australia by brothers Mark and Luke Finn as venue management software for entertainment and attractions businesses. The company raised $78.6M from Insight Partners and expanded to serve 1,500 venues in 30 countries.
Roller.summary_abuse: ROLLER is a venue management platform for attractions and entertainment centers with no notable abuse signal. It serves venue operators with booking and point-of-sale tools and does not expose a consumer-facing hosting surface suitable for fraud.

Rollick.history: Rollick was founded in 2017 in Austin, Texas by Jason Nierman and Amit Maheshwari as a lead management platform connecting manufacturers and dealers in powersports, RV, marine, and industrial equipment industries.
Rollick.summary_abuse: Rollick is a B2B lead management platform serving powersports and outdoor recreation industries with no notable abuse signal. It connects manufacturers and dealers and does not offer consumer-facing public hosting.

RomanCart.history: RomanCart is a long-running UK-based hosted shopping cart and internet marketing system primarily targeting small online retailers. It appears to have been in operation since the early 2000s with no notable ownership changes.
RomanCart.summary_abuse: RomanCart is a small, older hosted ecommerce cart with no documented significant abuse incidents. Its small user base and UK-focused market reduce its attractiveness for bulk fraudulent storefront creation.

Ronin Sport.history: Ronin Sport is a sports TV data provider offering data services for broadcast and sports engagement. Very limited public history is available.
Ronin Sport.summary_abuse: Ronin Sport is a niche sports data platform with no documented abuse history. It provides TV data services to sports broadcasters and does not expose a consumer-facing public hosting surface.

Rontar.history: Rontar is an ad management and retargeting platform with limited publicly documented history.
Rontar.summary_abuse: Rontar is an ad network and retargeting platform. Ad networks carry a moderate abuse risk as their ad delivery infrastructure can be misused to serve malvertisements or redirect users to fraudulent sites. No specific documented incidents are associated with Rontar.

Roof.history: Roof.ai (roofai.com) is an AI-powered digital marketing platform for real estate companies. Limited public founding history is available.
Roof.summary_abuse: Roof is a real estate digital marketing platform with no documented abuse history. It serves real estate businesses and does not expose a general-purpose consumer-facing hosting surface.

Roojoom.history: Roojoom is an Israeli content marketing platform focused on guided browsing and content monetization. Limited public history is available beyond its operation in the content marketing space.
Roojoom.summary_abuse: Roojoom is a content marketing platform with no documented abuse history. It focuses on B2B content monetization rather than consumer-facing hosting.

Roompot.history: Roompot is a Dutch holiday park operator and management company that has been in operation since the 1960s. It manages and operates holiday parks and campsites primarily in the Netherlands and other European countries.
Roompot.summary_abuse: Roompot is a Dutch holiday park operator with no notable technology-related abuse signal. It operates a booking website for its own properties and has a limited software technology surface relevant to fraud or phishing.

Rosana.history: Rosana is a conversational marketing automation SaaS that converts customer interactions into business outcomes. It is a recent AI-augmented product with limited public history.
Rosana.summary_abuse: Rosana is a conversational marketing automation platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool with no documented history of exploitation.

Rosti.history: Rosti is a Czech web hosting provider primarily serving Czech and Slovak developers, offering managed hosting for Python, Node.js, and PHP applications. Limited public founding history is available.
Rosti.summary_abuse: Rosti is a Czech web hosting provider with a modest abuse risk typical of shared hosting services. Like all web hosts, it could potentially host malicious content, though its small regional size limits its attractiveness as an abuse target. No specific documented incidents are publicly associated.

Rotic.history: Rotic is a conversion chatbot SaaS for capturing leads and booking meetings. It is a recent AI-augmented product with limited public history.
Rotic.summary_abuse: Rotic is a chatbot platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a small lead-capture tool with no documented history of exploitation.

RoundCube.history: RoundCube was founded in 2005 as a free and open-source web-based IMAP email client. It became one of the most widely deployed webmail solutions globally and is frequently bundled with web hosting control panels like cPanel. The RoundCube Foundation manages its development.
RoundCube.summary_abuse: RoundCube is a widely deployed open-source webmail client. Because it is self-hosted by millions of web hosting providers, any security vulnerabilities in RoundCube have historically been targeted for exploitation — notably, a 2023 zero-day (CVE-2023-43770) was weaponized by state-sponsored groups for credential harvesting. The project's security response history is generally reasonable, and vulnerabilities are typically patched promptly.

Route.history: Route was co-founded in 2019 by Evan Walker and Mike Moreno as a package tracking and shipping protection platform for e-commerce. The company raised $340M in total funding and achieved a $1.4 billion valuation with its Series C in 2024. It provides post-purchase protection including licensed shipping insurance.
Route.summary_abuse: Route is a shipping tracking and package protection platform used by e-commerce merchants. As a consumer-facing platform processing package claims and payment information, it requires standard fraud controls for abuse such as false claims. No documented widespread fraudulent misuse of Route's platform is known.

Roya.history: Roya is a US-based digital platform for online presence management and CMS. Very limited public founding history is available.
Roya.summary_abuse: Roya is a small website and CMS platform with no documented abuse history.

Royal Mail.history: Royal Mail is a British postal service with roots dating back to 1516, though the modern company was incorporated in 2001 from Royal Mail Holdings. Privatized in 2013 via a controversial IPO. Acquired by EP Group (Daniel Kretinsky) in 2024.
Royal Mail.summary_abuse: Royal Mail brand is frequently impersonated in smishing attacks sending fake delivery notifications to extract payment credentials. These attacks are perpetrated by external threat actors, not Royal Mail. Royal Mail publishes active warnings about such scams.

rSchoolToday.history: rSchoolToday is a US K-12 school activities management platform primarily serving high school athletic departments. It is the leading software for US high school athletic scheduling and has been acquired by PlayOn Sports.
rSchoolToday.summary_abuse: rSchoolToday is a school administration platform for K-12 athletic scheduling with no notable abuse signal. It serves verified K-12 school customers and has no consumer-facing features that would attract fraudsters.

Rspack.history: Rspack was developed by ByteDance's Web Infra team and open-sourced in March 2023. Written in Rust, it offers webpack-compatible API with significantly faster build times (up to 23x over webpack). ByteDance uses it internally for TikTok, Douyin, Lark, and Coze. Version 1.0 was released in October 2024.
Rspack.summary_abuse: Rspack is a JavaScript bundler tool used in the development build pipeline with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Rspress.history: Rspress is a static site generator built on Rspack, developed by the ByteDance Web Infra team and released as open-source in 2023. It supports MDX, full-text search, internationalization, and customizable themes. It is part of the broader Rsbuild/Rspack ecosystem.
Rspress.summary_abuse: Rspress is a static site generator developer tool with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

RSS.history: RSS (Really Simple Syndication) was first developed by Netscape in 1999 with RSS 0.90, later evolved through competing versions. RSS 2.0, the most widely adopted version, was developed by Dave Winer and released in 2002 under a Creative Commons license. The format became the dominant syndication standard for blogs, podcasts, and news sites, though its relevance declined somewhat after the shutdown of Google Reader in 2013.
RSS.summary_abuse: RSS is a foundational web syndication standard with no direct abuse signal. It is a passive data format used to distribute content and has no consumer-facing attack surface. Malicious RSS feeds could distribute spam content but the format itself is neutral infrastructure.

RTB House.history: RTB House was founded in 2012 in Warsaw, Poland, providing deep learning-powered retargeting and programmatic advertising solutions. The company grew to over $474M ARR and serves major brands and agencies globally, operating largely bootstrapped.
RTB House.summary_abuse: RTB House is a programmatic retargeting platform that collects extensive cross-site behavioural data on users, which carries inherent privacy risks. Like other ad networks it can be used to serve malvertising campaigns, but RTB House is primarily an enterprise B2B service with brand clients and operates with standard ad-industry anti-abuse measures.

Rtoaster.history: Rtoaster is a Japanese personalization and recommendation engine platform developed by BrainPad Inc., a data analytics company. It is primarily used by Japanese enterprise clients.
Rtoaster.summary_abuse: Rtoaster is a B2B personalization platform serving Japanese enterprises with no notable abuse signal. It does not expose consumer-facing public hosting surfaces.

Rual.history: Rual is a Dutch software development platform focused on tailor-made digital solutions. Very limited public history is available.
Rual.summary_abuse: Rual is a small Dutch custom software development platform with no documented abuse history.

Rubedo.history: Rubedo is an open-source PHP CMS built on Zend Framework, MongoDB, Elasticsearch, and AngularJS, developed by WebTales (a French company). The project has been inactive for several years with no recent releases, indicating it is effectively abandoned.
Rubedo.summary_abuse: Rubedo is an abandoned open-source PHP CMS with no notable abuse signal. As with any self-hosted CMS, outdated installations could pose security risks but there are no specific documented abuse patterns.

Rubic.history: Rubic is a Norwegian association management software for organizations managing memberships and events. Very limited public history is available.
Rubic.summary_abuse: Rubic is a niche Norwegian association management platform with no documented abuse history. It serves member organizations and does not provide a consumer-facing public hosting surface.

Rubicon Project.history: Rubicon Project was founded in 2007 as an advertising automation and SSP (supply-side platform) for publishers. It went public in April 2014. In 2020, Rubicon Project merged with Telaria and rebranded as Magnite, the largest independent sell-side advertising platform. The Rubicon Project brand lives on as part of Magnite's offering.
Rubicon Project.summary_abuse: Rubicon Project (now Magnite) is a major advertising SSP that processes billions of ad impressions. As a large ad exchange, it has historically been associated with malvertising incidents where bad actors inject malicious ads through programmatic channels. The platform has an IAB-certified ads.txt and TAG compliance, but ad tech ecosystems broadly remain vectors for malvertising at scale.

Ruby.history: Ruby created by Yukihiro Matsumoto in Japan, released 1995, designed for programmer happiness. Gained global prominence via Ruby on Rails in 2004.
Ruby.summary_abuse: Ruby is a programming language with no notable abuse signal. It has no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudulent use.

Ruby Chat.history: Ruby Chat is the live chat product arm of Ruby, the virtual receptionist company founded in 2003 in Portland, Oregon. In 2015, founder Jill Nelson sold a majority stake to Updata Partners. In 2019, Ruby acquired ProfessionalChats to expand into live chat services.
Ruby Chat.summary_abuse: Ruby Chat is a live chat managed service for small businesses with no notable abuse signal. It is a human-staffed service, not an autonomous chatbot platform, and does not expose a public hosting surface for fraud.

Ruby on Rails.history: Ruby on Rails was created by David Heinemeier Hansson (DHH) at 37signals and first released in 2004. It popularized convention-over-configuration and became one of the most influential web frameworks ever created. The framework is maintained by a core team and remains widely used despite increasing competition from Node.js and Python frameworks.
Ruby on Rails.summary_abuse: Ruby on Rails is a foundational web framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool used to build web applications with no consumer-facing surface itself.

Ruby Receptionists.history: Ruby Receptionists was founded in 2003 by Jill Nelson in Portland, Oregon as a virtual answering service for small businesses. In 2015, Nelson sold a majority stake to private equity firm Updata Partners for $38.8 million. In 2019, the company acquired ProfessionalChats to expand into live chat. In 2022 it was acquired again.
Ruby Receptionists.summary_abuse: Ruby Receptionists is a human-staffed virtual receptionist service with no notable abuse signal. It does not provide a consumer-facing hosting surface and is not a typical vector for phishing or fraud.

Rudderstack.history: RudderStack was founded in 2019 by Soumyadeb Mitra as an open-source customer data platform targeting developers. It raised $82M total including a $56M Series B from Insight Partners in 2022. The platform uses a warehouse-first architecture and is positioned as an open-source alternative to Segment.
Rudderstack.summary_abuse: RudderStack is a customer data infrastructure platform with no notable abuse signal. It collects and routes customer behavioral data for analytics purposes, and as a developer-facing tool with no consumer-facing hosted content, it is not a typical fraud vector.

Ruffle.history: Ruffle is an open-source Flash Player emulator written in Rust, begun around 2018 by community developers to preserve Flash content after Adobe deprecated Flash Player in December 2020. It is maintained by an open community with no corporate backing.
Ruffle.summary_abuse: Ruffle is a Flash emulator used to preserve legacy Flash content in browsers. While Flash-based content has historically been a significant malware vector, Ruffle itself is a legitimate OSS project. Malicious Flash content could potentially run through Ruffle, though modern browsers and security policies significantly limit this risk.

Ruler Analytics.history: Ruler Analytics is a UK-based sales and marketing attribution platform founded around 2014 that tracks leads from first touch through to conversion, using call tracking and multi-touch attribution.
Ruler Analytics.summary_abuse: Ruler Analytics is a marketing attribution and call-tracking platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B analytics tool that does not expose consumer-facing hosting.

Rumble.history: Rumble was founded on October 30, 2013 by Chris Pavlovski in Ontario, Canada as a video platform alternative to YouTube. It gained rapid growth among right-leaning audiences and went public via SPAC in 2022. The platform became known for hosting content creators banned or demonetized by YouTube.
Rumble.summary_abuse: Rumble has a documented pattern of lax content moderation, hosting conspiracy theorists, misinformation spreaders, and individuals banned from other major platforms including Alex Jones and Andrew Tate. The platform markets itself as a free-speech alternative, which has attracted both legitimate users and those seeking to avoid content moderation. Researchers have documented significant misinformation and extremist content on the platform. The free account tier with video hosting enables rapid deployment of harmful content without the moderation controls present on mainstream platforms.

Rumvision.history: Rumvision is a front-end performance monitoring service focused on Real User Monitoring (RUM) for web applications. Very limited public founding history is available.
Rumvision.summary_abuse: Rumvision is a web performance monitoring tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a technical B2B tool with no consumer-facing hosting surface.

RunKit.history: RunKit was originally built as Tonic in 2014. In September 2016, Stripe acquired Tonic and rebranded it as RunKit, integrating it as a developer sandbox for the Node.js / npm ecosystem. It is now part of Stripe's developer tooling infrastructure.
RunKit.summary_abuse: RunKit is a sandboxed JavaScript execution environment owned by Stripe. Like all executable code sandboxes, it carries a risk of being used to prototype malicious scripts, but Stripe's security posture and the developer-focused nature of the platform mean legitimate use dominates significantly. No specific high-profile abuse campaigns are documented.

Rust.history: Rust was initiated by Graydon Hoare at Mozilla Research around 2006-2010 and first officially released in 2010. Mozilla sponsored development until version 1.0 in 2015. In 2021, the Rust Foundation was established as an independent nonprofit to steward the language, with founding members including Mozilla, Microsoft, Google, AWS, and Huawei.
Rust.summary_abuse: Rust is a systems programming language with no notable abuse signal. It is increasingly used for security-critical software and malware by sophisticated threat actors, but this reflects its performance properties rather than a vendor surface issue.

Ruttl.history: Ruttl is an Indian-founded visual feedback and bug reporting platform launched around 2020, aimed at web design and development teams. It remains an independent startup.
Ruttl.summary_abuse: Ruttl is a website feedback and annotation tool with no notable abuse signal. It serves design and development teams and does not expose a general public hosting surface.

Ruzuku.history: Ruzuku is a US-based online course creation and community platform founded around 2012. It remains an independent bootstrapped product targeting educators and instructors.
Ruzuku.summary_abuse: Ruzuku is an online course platform with no notable abuse signal. It is used by legitimate educators and instructors and does not provide a consumer-facing free hosting surface commonly exploited for fraud.

RX Web Server.history: RX Web Server is a niche server software product with limited public documentation.
RX Web Server.summary_abuse: RX Web Server has no notable abuse signal based on available information.

RxJS.history: RxJS is the JavaScript implementation of ReactiveX, a cross-language reactive programming library. It was developed primarily by Microsoft and the Angular team, becoming a core dependency of Angular. Version 5 was a significant rewrite. The library is widely used in enterprise Angular applications.
RxJS.summary_abuse: RxJS is a reactive programming library with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool used in frontend applications with no consumer-facing surface.

Rybbit.history: Rybbit is an open-source, cookieless web analytics platform launched in 2024 as a privacy-first alternative to Google Analytics. Built on ClickHouse, it reached 11,000+ GitHub stars quickly and is trusted by over 4,000 organizations. It offers both cloud-hosted and self-hosted options under AGPL-3.0.
Rybbit.summary_abuse: Rybbit is a privacy-focused open-source analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. Its cookieless design and GDPR compliance mean it is used specifically to reduce tracking footprint, and it has no documented history of exploitation for fraud or malware.

Ryviu.history: Ryviu is a product review app for Shopify stores, primarily built and maintained by a Vietnamese development team. Limited public founding history is available.
Ryviu.summary_abuse: Ryviu is a product review app for ecommerce stores with no documented major abuse incidents. Review platforms can carry review manipulation and fake-review risk, but no specific abuse campaigns are associated with Ryviu itself.

Ryzeo.history: Ryzeo is an AI-powered product recommendation and personalization SaaS for ecommerce. It is a small startup with no notable ownership events.
Ryzeo.summary_abuse: Ryzeo is a product recommendation platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing tool with no documented history of exploitation.
